skill.name: implementing-code
skill.description: Guides an AI coding agent through code investigation, implementation, and git commits using existing project conventions.
skill.summary: Implement code changes and create conventional commits with project-aware workflow steps.
skill.author: 1gy

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution and weak cryptography patterns, but review found no cryptographic implementation and no malicious behavior. The shell examples are limited to git add and git commit workflow instructions, so the remaining risk is normal repository write and commit activity.

low_findings.title: Restricted Git Command Execution
low_findings.description: The skill instructs use of git add and git commit. This is expected for a code implementation workflow, but it can modify repository state and trigger project git hooks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and limited to git workflow operations. Risk remains because git commands can change local repository state, but no arbitrary shell execution or exfiltration is shown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography on the description and commit placeholder lines. Review found only prose and placeholder text, with no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain descriptive metadata or conventional commit placeholders. There is no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, encryption, signing, or password storage logic.

dangerous_patterns.title: Git Shell Command Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis flagged shell command examples. Review confirmed these are documented git add and git commit commands for committing completed changes.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command block is clearly present and semantically tied to the stated commit workflow. It is not malicious, but it justifies retaining the external_commands risk factor.

content.value_statement: Implementation work often fails when agents skip project context or commit rules. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a simple workflow for investigating code, making focused edits, and creating conventional commits.
actual_capabilities: Directs the agent to inspect related code before editing.
Encourages implementation that follows existing project patterns.
Defines a conventional commit format for completed changes.
Lists common commit types for features, fixes, docs, tests, and chores.
Adds coordination rules for parallel work on separate files.
Specifies a completion report with changed files, summary, and commit hash.
limitations: Does not include language-specific implementation guidance.
Does not define testing commands or quality gates.
Assumes the repository already has clear project conventions.
Uses git commands, so repository write access is required.

use_cases.title: Ship a Small Feature
use_cases.description: Use the workflow to inspect existing code, add a focused feature, and commit it with a conventional message.

use_cases.title: Fix a Reproducible Bug
use_cases.description: Guide an agent through targeted investigation, minimal code changes, and a clear fix commit.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Parallel Implementation
use_cases.description: Use the parallel execution notes to avoid editing the same files across simultaneous coding tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Implement a Basic Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement the requested change. Inspect the related files first, follow existing patterns, and report the changed files.
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a small code update

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Bug
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the cause of this bug, make the smallest reliable fix, and commit the change with a conventional commit message.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate bug fix with commit creation

prompt_templates.title: Add a Feature with Tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Add this feature using the existing architecture. Include relevant tests if the project has a matching test pattern, then commit the result.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced feature work in an established codebase

prompt_templates.title: Implement in Parallel
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement this task without editing files assigned to other parallel workers. Stop and report any conflict before changing shared files.

output_examples.input: Add validation to the user settings form and commit the result.
output_examples.output: Changed the settings form validation, added matching coverage, and created a fix commit with a concise summary.

output_examples.input: Fix the failing password reset flow without touching unrelated files.
output_examples.output: Identified the reset token handling issue, updated the targeted module, verified the relevant flow, and reported the commit hash.

output_examples.input: Implement this task while another worker owns the billing files.
output_examples.output: Completed changes outside the billing area and reported that no parallel file conflicts were found.

best_practices: Provide a clear purpose and deliverable before starting implementation.
Review project-specific instructions before choosing a commit message.
Keep edits focused on files needed for the requested change.
anti_patterns: Do not skip code investigation before editing.
Do not modify files assigned to another parallel task.
Do not create vague commit messages that hide the change type.

faq.question: What does this skill help an agent do?
faq.answer: It helps an agent inspect code, implement changes, and create conventional git commits.

faq.question: Does it run arbitrary shell commands?
faq.answer: The documented command examples are limited to git add and git commit.

faq.question: Can it handle bug fixes and features?
faq.answer: Yes. The trigger description includes implementation tasks, feature additions, and bug fixes.

faq.question: Does it include testing instructions?
faq.answer: No. It does not define test commands, so users should provide project-specific test guidance.

faq.question: How should commits be formatted?
faq.answer: It recommends conventional commit messages with a type and short description.

faq.question: Is it safe for parallel coding work?
faq.answer: It includes basic conflict rules, but users still need clear file ownership across parallel tasks.
